title: BEM-916 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM916_WORKER_UNIFIED_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-916 WORKER UNIFIED SYSTEM IMPROVEMENT PROTOCOL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator clarification is correct. Telegram must not be a detached protocol. It should be a phase and mechanism inside the previous BEM-914 system improvement protocol.
- BEM-915 should be treated as input material for the Telegram Operator Interface section of the unified protocol.
- The unified protocol must preserve the original BEM-914 remediation roadmap and add Telegram as an integrated operator-facing channel.
- The protocol must explicitly state that Telegram uses the same policy gate, dispatch queue, managed channel, proof policy and role hierarchy.
- The Telegram layer should not bypass Curator/Director/General Director routing.
- The operator must be able to submit tasks and receive meaningful reports from Curator, Director and General Director, not only hourly no-change reports.
Autonomous scope:
- Protocol writing, schemas, command grammar, routing tables, deterministic parser stub, E2E selftest design, report templates, ZIP/DOCX generation.
Operator gates:
- Real Telegram token/secrets, production webhook mutation, issue #31 write permission, schedule trigger, always-on daemon, real pilot selection, paid API and LLM-agent runtime model.
Recommendation:
- Approve unified protocol with notes.
- Status: UNIFIED_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES.
No issue comments.
